linting.notes: The project uses pre-commit hooks in CI via the develop container. Pre-commit runs golangci-lint and prettier. The develop container has all pre-commit environments pre-installed to avoid Go version conflicts.

testing.notes: Tests are in proxy/*_test.go. Run tests by package (./proxy) not by file. The project requires Go 1.25.5 via go.mod, which requires GOTOOLCHAIN=auto to download the correct version.

ci.notes: The test workflow runs on PRs to main and release branches. It uses a containerized development environment (make build.develop, ./scripts/develop.sh) to ensure consistent tooling. The develop container has Go 1.25, pre-commit, golangci-lint, and all dependencies pre-installed.

build.notes: Multi-stage Dockerfile: develop stage (ubi9/go-toolset:1.25) has all dev tools, build stage compiles the binary, runtime stage (ubi9/ubi-micro) contains only the binary. The develop container workflow (make build.develop, ./scripts/develop.sh) is the recommended dev environment.

conventions.test_naming: Go standard: Test* functions in *_test.go files
conventions.notes: Uses Go modules. Generated code in gen/ is excluded from linting.

test_commands.notes: Passed: proxy package 64.1% coverage, all tests passed

container_recipe.notes: The go.mod specifies go 1.25.5, which requires GOTOOLCHAIN=auto to download. The gen/ package has generated code and shows 'no such tool covdata' warning but this is harmless. Running single test files doesn't work; run by package instead (e.g., go test ./proxy -run TestRESTResponse).

results.notes: Dependencies downloaded successfully. Required GOTOOLCHAIN=auto to download Go 1.25.5.
Tests passed successfully. 64.1% code coverage in proxy package. Warning about 'no such tool covdata' for gen/ package is harmless (generated code).

validation.summary: install OK, lint OK (0 issues), tests OK (all passed, 64.1% coverage)

gaps: The go.mod specifies Go 1.25.5, which is newer than commonly available base images. Requires GOTOOLCHAIN=auto workaround.
Pre-commit hooks cannot be installed directly in a basic golang container due to Go version conflicts when installing golangci-lint from source.
The project's intended workflow uses a custom develop container (make build.develop) which pre-installs all tools. Direct validation requires installing golangci-lint binary separately.
No coverage threshold configured. Current coverage is 64.1%.
Tests cannot be run by individual file, only by package.
